benchmark_honesty_caveat: Autonomous second-round repair-context selector output. Hidden aligned target Lean declarations, statements, names, and proofs are not included. Mathlib signatures here are included only after Lean #check hydration.

checked_signatures.name: Matrix.det_of_lowerTriangular
checked_signatures.signature:
Matrix.det_of_lowerTriangular.{v, u_3} {m : Type u_3} {R : Type v} [CommRing R] [DecidableEq m] [Fintype m]
  [LinearOrder m] (M : Matrix m m R) (h : M.BlockTriangular ⇑OrderDual.toDual) : M.det = ∏ i, M i i
checked_signatures.why_needed: To prove the lower triangular case directly.

missing_or_uncertain_context.item: Whether the hidden target expects a single theorem covering both cases or two separate theorems.

proof_strategy_notes.failure_analysis: The generated theorem `det_transpose'` compiles successfully and covers the source theorem `thm.det.transp`. No repair needed.
proof_strategy_notes.proof_strategy_note: No repair needed; the theorem compiles.

proof_strategy_notes.failure_analysis: The generated theorem `det_triangular` compiles successfully but only covers the upper triangular case via `Matrix.det_of_upperTriangular`. The source theorem `thm.det.triang` explicitly states 'lower-triangular or upper-triangular'. The generated Lean file is missing coverage for the lower triangular case. Since the unit is in `source_coverage_review_unit_keys`, we need to select context to add a theorem for the lower triangular case.
proof_strategy_notes.proof_strategy_note: Add a second theorem `det_triangular_lower` (or extend the existing theorem) that uses `Matrix.det_of_lowerTriangular` for the lower triangular case. The existing `det_triangular` covers the upper triangular case; the lower triangular case can be proved by applying `det_transpose'` and `det_of_upperTriangular` to the transpose, or directly using `det_of_lowerTriangular`. The visible context already includes `det_transpose'` and `open Matrix`.

proof_strategy_notes.failure_analysis: The generated theorems `det_permute_rows` and `det_permute_cols` compile successfully and cover both equations of the source corollary `cor.det.sig-row-col`. No repair needed.
proof_strategy_notes.proof_strategy_note: No repair needed; the theorems compile.
